title: Jesus the Promised Saviour
unitGoal: Students practise the Gospel timeline and learn that Jesus is the promised Saviour at the centre of the whole Bible.

lessons.title: The Promised Saviour Arrives
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise incarnation, fulfilled promises, and Jesus' birth.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise incarnation, fulfilled promises, and Jesus' birth.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: The Promised Saviour Arrives Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand Jesus' birth as God's promised rescue arriving.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: The Promised Saviour Arrives
steps.text: This lesson practises incarnation, fulfilled promises, and Jesus' birth. Jesus is the promised Saviour, King, and Son of God. Scripture connection: Luke 1:26-38; Luke 2:1-20; John 1:1-14. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: The Word Became Flesh
steps.prompt: John 1 teaches that the Word became flesh, meaning...
choices: the Son of God truly became human
God revealed truth without truly entering human life
Jesus became God's Son only after His birth
steps.correctAnswer: the Son of God truly became human
steps.feedback: Correct. The eternal Son truly took on human nature.
steps.hint: The eternal Son truly took on human nature.

steps.title: Virgin Birth
steps.prompt: Jesus' birth showed...
choices: God's promised Saviour coming by God's power
faithful people arranging the promise by themselves
political rulers setting the timing of God's promise
steps.correctAnswer: God's promised Saviour coming by God's power
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus' birth was God's work and fulfilled His promises.
steps.hint: Jesus' birth was God's work and fulfilled His promises.

steps.title: True God and True Man
steps.prompt: Jesus is true God and true man.
choices: True
False
steps.feedback: Correct. The incarnation means the Son of God truly became human without ceasing to be God.
steps.hint: The incarnation means the Son of God truly became human without ceasing to be God.

steps.title: Son of David
steps.prompt: Calling Jesus the Son of David connects Him to...
choices: God's kingdom promise to David
a family line important mainly for national pride
a promise limited to one ancient kingdom
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus fulfills the promise of the King from David's line.
steps.hint: Jesus fulfills the promise of the King from David's line.

steps.title: Good News
steps.prompt: The angels announced Jesus' birth as...
choices: good news of great joy
good news mainly about peace in the empire
news meant only for people already considered important
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus' birth is good news for God's people and beyond.
steps.hint: Jesus' birth is good news for God's people and beyond.

steps.title: Shepherds
steps.prompt: The shepherds' response teaches that good news should lead to...
choices: faith, witness, and praise
quiet agreement without public praise
curiosity without changed response
steps.feedback: Correct. They went, saw, told, and glorified God.
steps.hint: They went, saw, told, and glorified God.

questions.prompt: John 1 teaches that the Word became flesh, meaning...
choices: the Son of God truly became human
God revealed truth without truly entering human life
Jesus became God's Son only after His birth
questions.correctAnswer: the Son of God truly became human
questions.reviewNote: The eternal Son truly took on human nature.

questions.prompt: Jesus' birth showed...
choices: God's promised Saviour coming by God's power
faithful people arranging the promise by themselves
political rulers setting the timing of God's promise
questions.correctAnswer: God's promised Saviour coming by God's power
questions.reviewNote: Jesus' birth was God's work and fulfilled His promises.

questions.prompt: Jesus is true God and true man.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The incarnation means the Son of God truly became human without ceasing to be God.

questions.prompt: Calling Jesus the Son of David connects Him to...
choices: God's kingdom promise to David
a family line important mainly for national pride
a promise limited to one ancient kingdom
questions.reviewNote: Jesus fulfills the promise of the King from David's line.

questions.prompt: The angels announced Jesus' birth as...
choices: good news of great joy
good news mainly about peace in the empire
news meant only for people already considered important
questions.reviewNote: Jesus' birth is good news for God's people and beyond.

lessons.title: Jesus Announces the Kingdom
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise repentance, faith, discipleship, and the kingdom of God.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise repentance, faith, discipleship, and the kingdom of God.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Jesus Announces the Kingdom Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand Jesus' kingdom announcement and call to follow Him.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Jesus Announces the Kingdom
steps.text: This lesson practises repentance, faith, discipleship, and the kingdom of God. God is faithful to His promises, and His people need a true and faithful King. Scripture connection: Mark 1:14-20; Matthew 5:1-16; Luke 4:16-21. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: Jesus' Announcement
steps.prompt: Jesus announced, 'The kingdom of God is at hand,' and called people to...
choices: repent and believe the gospel
clean up outward habits before coming to God
support God's kingdom without repentance
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus called for repentance and faith.
steps.hint: Jesus called for repentance and faith.

steps.title: Repentance
steps.prompt: Repentance means...
choices: turning from sin toward God
feeling sorry while keeping sin protected
changing outward habits while guarding the heart
steps.feedback: Correct. Repentance includes a real turning to God.
steps.hint: Repentance includes a real turning to God.

steps.title: Faith
steps.prompt: Faith means trusting Jesus, not merely knowing facts about Him.
choices: True
False
steps.feedback: Correct. Biblical faith includes trust.
steps.hint: Biblical faith includes trust.

steps.title: Follow Me
steps.prompt: When Jesus called disciples, He called them to...
choices: follow Him and learn His way
add His teaching to their plans without surrender
follow Him mainly for a better public position
steps.correctAnswer: follow Him and learn His way
steps.feedback: Correct. Disciples follow and learn from Jesus.
steps.hint: Disciples follow and learn from Jesus.

steps.title: Salt and Light
steps.prompt: Jesus called His followers salt and light to show they should...
choices: live as witnesses under God's rule
keep faith useful but mostly private
prove worth by visible religious success
steps.correctAnswer: live as witnesses under God's rule
steps.feedback: Correct. Disciples are to reflect God's light in the world.
steps.hint: Disciples are to reflect God's light in the world.

steps.title: Kingdom of God
steps.prompt: The kingdom of God means...
choices: God's saving rule breaking into the world through Jesus
God's rule shown mostly through political control
an inner hope that does not change loyalty
steps.correctAnswer: God's saving rule breaking into the world through Jesus
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus brings God's saving reign.
steps.hint: Jesus brings God's saving reign.

questions.prompt: Jesus announced, 'The kingdom of God is at hand,' and called people to...
choices: repent and believe the gospel
clean up outward habits before coming to God
support God's kingdom without repentance
questions.reviewNote: Jesus called for repentance and faith.

questions.prompt: Repentance means...
choices: turning from sin toward God
feeling sorry while keeping sin protected
changing outward habits while guarding the heart
questions.reviewNote: Repentance includes a real turning to God.

questions.prompt: Faith means trusting Jesus, not merely knowing facts about Him.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Biblical faith includes trust.

questions.prompt: When Jesus called disciples, He called them to...
choices: follow Him and learn His way
add His teaching to their plans without surrender
follow Him mainly for a better public position
questions.correctAnswer: follow Him and learn His way
questions.reviewNote: Disciples follow and learn from Jesus.

questions.prompt: Jesus called His followers salt and light to show they should...
choices: live as witnesses under God's rule
keep faith useful but mostly private
prove worth by visible religious success
questions.correctAnswer: live as witnesses under God's rule
questions.reviewNote: Disciples are to reflect God's light in the world.

lessons.title: Miracles Show Who Jesus Is
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise Jesus' authority over sickness, storms, evil, death, and creation.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise Jesus' authority over sickness, storms, evil, death, and creation.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Miracles Show Who Jesus Is Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand how Jesus' miracles reveal His identity and mission.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Miracles Show Who Jesus Is
steps.text: This lesson practises Jesus' authority over sickness, storms, evil, death, and creation. Jesus is the promised Saviour, King, and Son of God. Scripture connection: Mark 2:1-12; Mark 4:35-41; John 11:1-44. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: Forgiveness and Healing
steps.prompt: When Jesus healed the paralytic, He also showed authority to...
choices: forgive sins
prove His authority without confronting sin
make people admire power apart from repentance
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus connected the healing with His authority to forgive.
steps.hint: Jesus connected the healing with His authority to forgive.

steps.title: Storm
steps.prompt: When Jesus calmed the storm, the disciples wondered...
choices: who He was, because wind and sea obeyed Him
whether nature was outside His authority
whether experienced people should guide Him first
steps.correctAnswer: who He was, because wind and sea obeyed Him
steps.feedback: Correct. The miracle revealed Jesus' authority over creation.
steps.hint: The miracle revealed Jesus' authority over creation.

steps.title: Signs
steps.prompt: Jesus' miracles were signs pointing to who He is.
choices: True
False
steps.feedback: Correct. The miracles were not random displays; they revealed His identity.
steps.hint: The miracles were not random displays; they revealed His identity.

steps.title: Sickness and Suffering
steps.prompt: Jesus' healing miracles show both His power and His...
choices: compassion toward suffering people
power without personal compassion
concern for bodies without concern for souls
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus' miracles show authority and mercy.
steps.hint: Jesus' miracles show authority and mercy.

steps.title: Lazarus
steps.prompt: Raising Lazarus showed that Jesus has authority over...
choices: death
grief but not death itself
human limits that faith cannot face
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus called Lazarus from the tomb.
steps.hint: Jesus called Lazarus from the tomb.

steps.title: Right Response
steps.prompt: A right response to Jesus' miracles is to...
choices: trust and worship Him
admire His power without trusting His teaching
receive help from Him without worshiping Him
steps.feedback: Correct. The signs call people to trust Jesus.
steps.hint: The signs call people to trust Jesus.

quiz.title: Miracles Show Who Jesus Is Quiz

questions.prompt: When Jesus healed the paralytic, He also showed authority to...
choices: forgive sins
prove His authority without confronting sin
make people admire power apart from repentance
questions.reviewNote: Jesus connected the healing with His authority to forgive.

questions.prompt: When Jesus calmed the storm, the disciples wondered...
choices: who He was, because wind and sea obeyed Him
whether nature was outside His authority
whether experienced people should guide Him first
questions.correctAnswer: who He was, because wind and sea obeyed Him
questions.reviewNote: The miracle revealed Jesus' authority over creation.

questions.prompt: Jesus' miracles were signs pointing to who He is.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The miracles were not random displays; they revealed His identity.

questions.prompt: Jesus' healing miracles show both His power and His...
choices: compassion toward suffering people
power without personal compassion
concern for bodies without concern for souls
questions.reviewNote: Jesus' miracles show authority and mercy.

questions.prompt: Raising Lazarus showed that Jesus has authority over...
choices: death
grief but not death itself
human limits that faith cannot face
questions.reviewNote: Jesus called Lazarus from the tomb.

lessons.title: Parables Teach Truth
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise kingdom stories and heart responses to Jesus' teaching.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise kingdom stories and heart responses to Jesus' teaching.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Parables Teach Truth Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand how Jesus used parables to teach truth about God's kingdom.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Parables Teach Truth
steps.text: This lesson practises kingdom stories and heart responses to Jesus' teaching. Jesus is the promised Saviour, King, and Son of God. Scripture connection: Luke 10:25-37; Luke 15:11-32; Matthew 13:1-23. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: What Is a Parable?
steps.prompt: A parable is a story Jesus used to...
choices: teach truth about God's kingdom and human hearts
make truth sound clever instead of clear
make stories more important than repentance
steps.correctAnswer: teach truth about God's kingdom and human hearts
steps.feedback: Correct. Parables use story to teach deep truth.
steps.hint: Parables use story to teach deep truth.

steps.title: Listening
steps.prompt: Parables often reveal...
choices: how people respond to God's word
that imagination can replace obedience
that understanding truth matters less than enjoying the story
steps.correctAnswer: how people respond to God's word
steps.feedback: Correct. Parables often expose the heart.
steps.hint: Parables often expose the heart.

steps.title: Heart Response
steps.prompt: Jesus' parables call for listening, repentance, faith, and obedience.
choices: True
False
steps.feedback: Correct. Parables are meant to be heard and responded to.
steps.hint: Parables are meant to be heard and responded to.

steps.title: Neighbour Love
steps.prompt: The Good Samaritan teaches that neighbour love is...
choices: active mercy toward the person in need
kindness only when it is socially expected
feeling sympathy without costly action
steps.correctAnswer: active mercy toward the person in need
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus showed mercy crossing social boundaries.
steps.hint: Jesus showed mercy crossing social boundaries.

steps.title: Mercy and Repentance
steps.prompt: The parable of the lost son shows...
choices: repentance, mercy, and the father's welcome
forgiveness without repentance or restoration
outward obedience without a resentful heart being serious
steps.correctAnswer: repentance, mercy, and the father's welcome
steps.feedback: Correct. The father welcomes the returning son with mercy.
steps.hint: The father welcomes the returning son with mercy.

steps.title: Good Soil
steps.prompt: In the parable of the sower, good soil pictures...
choices: a heart that receives the word and bears fruit
quick interest that avoids deep trust
people who understand words but protect distractions
steps.correctAnswer: a heart that receives the word and bears fruit
steps.feedback: Correct. The good soil hears, receives, and bears fruit.
steps.hint: The good soil hears, receives, and bears fruit.

questions.prompt: A parable is a story Jesus used to...
choices: teach truth about God's kingdom and human hearts
make truth sound clever instead of clear
make stories more important than repentance
questions.correctAnswer: teach truth about God's kingdom and human hearts
questions.reviewNote: Parables use story to teach deep truth.

questions.prompt: Parables often reveal...
choices: how people respond to God's word
that imagination can replace obedience
that understanding truth matters less than enjoying the story
questions.correctAnswer: how people respond to God's word
questions.reviewNote: Parables often expose the heart.

questions.prompt: Jesus' parables call for listening, repentance, faith, and obedience.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Parables are meant to be heard and responded to.

questions.prompt: The Good Samaritan teaches that neighbour love is...
choices: active mercy toward the person in need
kindness only when it is socially expected
feeling sympathy without costly action
questions.correctAnswer: active mercy toward the person in need
questions.reviewNote: Jesus showed mercy crossing social boundaries.

questions.prompt: The parable of the lost son shows...
choices: repentance, mercy, and the father's welcome
forgiveness without repentance or restoration
outward obedience without a resentful heart being serious
questions.correctAnswer: repentance, mercy, and the father's welcome
questions.reviewNote: The father welcomes the returning son with mercy.

lessons.title: Jesus and the Law
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise fulfillment, heart obedience, holiness, love for God, and love for neighbour.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise fulfillment, heart obedience, holiness, love for God, and love for neighbour.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Jesus and the Law Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand Jesus' teaching on the law, the heart, and true obedience.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Jesus and the Law
steps.text: This lesson practises fulfillment, heart obedience, holiness, love for God, and love for neighbour. Jesus is the promised Saviour, King, and Son of God. Scripture connection: Matthew 5:17-48; Matthew 22:34-40. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: Not Abolish
steps.prompt: Jesus said He came not to abolish the Law and Prophets but to...
choices: fulfill them
lower God's standard to outward habits
treat them as old customs with little authority
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus fulfills the Law and Prophets.
steps.hint: Jesus fulfills the Law and Prophets.

steps.title: Greater Righteousness
steps.prompt: Jesus taught that righteousness must go deeper than...
choices: outward appearance alone
the words of the Old Testament
the teaching of the prophets
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus exposed heart-level sin and obedience.
steps.hint: Jesus exposed heart-level sin and obedience.

steps.title: God's Word
steps.prompt: Jesus treated the Old Testament as God's true Word.
choices: True
False
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus honoured and fulfilled the Scriptures.
steps.hint: Jesus honoured and fulfilled the Scriptures.

steps.title: Anger and Murder
steps.prompt: Jesus connected murder with...
choices: anger and contempt in the heart
anger that seems hidden from other people
conflict only after it becomes public
steps.correctAnswer: anger and contempt in the heart
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus taught that sin begins in the heart.
steps.hint: Jesus taught that sin begins in the heart.

steps.title: Love Command
steps.prompt: Jesus summarized the law with...
choices: love God and love your neighbour
keep visible rules and avoid scandal
choose the rules that feel easiest to keep
steps.correctAnswer: love God and love your neighbour
steps.feedback: Correct. Love for God and neighbour summarizes the law.
steps.hint: Love for God and neighbour summarizes the law.

steps.title: True Obedience
steps.prompt: Jesus' teaching shows that true obedience...
choices: flows from a heart submitted to God
means outward success without heart change
is possible without forgiveness
steps.correctAnswer: flows from a heart submitted to God
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus calls for heart-level holiness.
steps.hint: Jesus calls for heart-level holiness.

questions.prompt: Jesus said He came not to abolish the Law and Prophets but to...
choices: fulfill them
lower God's standard to outward habits
treat them as old customs with little authority
questions.reviewNote: Jesus fulfills the Law and Prophets.

questions.prompt: Jesus taught that righteousness must go deeper than...
choices: outward appearance alone
the words of the Old Testament
the teaching of the prophets
questions.reviewNote: Jesus exposed heart-level sin and obedience.

questions.prompt: Jesus treated the Old Testament as God's true Word.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Jesus honoured and fulfilled the Scriptures.

questions.prompt: Jesus connected murder with...
choices: anger and contempt in the heart
anger that seems hidden from other people
conflict only after it becomes public
questions.correctAnswer: anger and contempt in the heart
questions.reviewNote: Jesus taught that sin begins in the heart.

questions.prompt: Jesus summarized the law with...
choices: love God and love your neighbour
keep visible rules and avoid scandal
choose the rules that feel easiest to keep
questions.correctAnswer: love God and love your neighbour
questions.reviewNote: Love for God and neighbour summarizes the law.

lessons.title: The Cross
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise sin, substitution, sacrifice, forgiveness, justice, and love at the cross.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise sin, substitution, sacrifice, forgiveness, justice, and love at the cross.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: The Cross Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand the cross as God's just and loving rescue through Jesus.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: The Cross
steps.text: This lesson practises sin, substitution, sacrifice, forgiveness, justice, and love at the cross. Jesus is the promised Saviour, King, and Son of God. Scripture connection: Mark 15:21-39; John 19:16-30; 1 Peter 2:24. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: Human Sin
steps.prompt: The cross shows that sin is...
choices: so serious that only God's rescue can deal with it
less serious than religious leaders claimed
serious, but solved mainly by human regret
steps.correctAnswer: so serious that only God's rescue can deal with it
steps.feedback: Correct. The cross shows sin's seriousness and God's rescue.
steps.hint: The cross shows sin's seriousness and God's rescue.

steps.title: Substitution
steps.prompt: Substitution means Jesus...
choices: stood in the place of sinners
suffered beside sinners but not for them
removed guilt without changing loyalty
steps.correctAnswer: stood in the place of sinners
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus bore sin for His people.
steps.hint: Jesus bore sin for His people.

steps.title: Love and Justice
steps.prompt: At the cross, God's love and justice are both shown.
choices: True
False
steps.feedback: Correct. The cross does not set love against justice.
steps.hint: The cross does not set love against justice.

steps.title: Lamb of God
steps.prompt: Calling Jesus the Lamb of God connects His death to...
choices: sacrifice and rescue
a rescue that skipped judgment
a symbol of courage more than sacrifice
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus is the true sacrifice for sin.
steps.hint: Jesus is the true sacrifice for sin.

steps.title: It Is Finished
steps.prompt: When Jesus said, 'It is finished,' He meant...
choices: His saving work was completed
His public ministry was over but not His saving work
human rulers had finished their part of the story
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus completed the work the Father gave Him.
steps.hint: Jesus completed the work the Father gave Him.

steps.title: Forgiveness
steps.prompt: Forgiveness through the cross means...
choices: sin is paid for by Christ, not excused as harmless
grace means sin can be treated lightly
guilt itself is what earns forgiveness
steps.correctAnswer: sin is paid for by Christ, not excused as harmless
steps.feedback: Correct. The cross gives real forgiveness because Christ paid for sin.
steps.hint: The cross gives real forgiveness because Christ paid for sin.

questions.prompt: The cross shows that sin is...
choices: so serious that only God's rescue can deal with it
less serious than religious leaders claimed
serious, but solved mainly by human regret
questions.correctAnswer: so serious that only God's rescue can deal with it
questions.reviewNote: The cross shows sin's seriousness and God's rescue.

questions.prompt: Substitution means Jesus...
choices: stood in the place of sinners
suffered beside sinners but not for them
removed guilt without changing loyalty
questions.correctAnswer: stood in the place of sinners
questions.reviewNote: Jesus bore sin for His people.

questions.prompt: At the cross, God's love and justice are both shown.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The cross does not set love against justice.

questions.prompt: Calling Jesus the Lamb of God connects His death to...
choices: sacrifice and rescue
a rescue that skipped judgment
a symbol of courage more than sacrifice
questions.reviewNote: Jesus is the true sacrifice for sin.

questions.prompt: When Jesus said, 'It is finished,' He meant...
choices: His saving work was completed
His public ministry was over but not His saving work
human rulers had finished their part of the story
questions.reviewNote: Jesus completed the work the Father gave Him.

lessons.title: Resurrection and Ascension
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise Jesus alive, victory, His reign, and His sending of His people.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise Jesus alive, victory, His reign, and His sending of His people.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Resurrection and Ascension Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand Jesus' resurrection and ascension as victory and hope.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Resurrection and Ascension
steps.text: This lesson practises Jesus alive, victory, His reign, and His sending of His people. Jesus is the promised Saviour, King, and Son of God. Scripture connection: Luke 24:1-53; Acts 1:1-11; 1 Corinthians 15:3-8. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: Empty Tomb
steps.prompt: The empty tomb means...
choices: Jesus truly rose from the dead
Jesus lived on only through His followers' courage
hope became only a memory of His example
steps.correctAnswer: Jesus truly rose from the dead
steps.feedback: Correct. The resurrection is real, bodily victory over death.
steps.hint: The resurrection is real, bodily victory over death.

steps.title: Witnesses
steps.prompt: The risen Jesus appeared to...
choices: real witnesses who saw Him alive
followers whose hope was mainly symbolic
people who could make the story easier to believe
steps.correctAnswer: real witnesses who saw Him alive
steps.feedback: Correct. The New Testament names witnesses of the risen Christ.
steps.hint: The New Testament names witnesses of the risen Christ.

steps.title: Victory
steps.prompt: Jesus' resurrection shows victory over sin and death.
choices: True
False
steps.feedback: Correct. The resurrection confirms Jesus' victory.
steps.hint: The resurrection confirms Jesus' victory.

steps.title: Jesus Reigns
steps.prompt: Jesus' ascension means He...
choices: reigns at the Father's right hand
moved away from earth and stopped ruling it
became present only as a memory or idea
steps.correctAnswer: reigns at the Father's right hand
steps.feedback: Correct. The ascended Christ reigns.
steps.hint: The ascended Christ reigns.

steps.title: Mission
steps.prompt: Before ascending, Jesus sent His disciples to...
choices: be His witnesses
wait until the nations became ready
begin with political reform before witness
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus sent His followers as witnesses.
steps.hint: Jesus sent His followers as witnesses.

steps.title: Christian Hope
steps.prompt: The resurrection gives Christians hope because...
choices: Jesus is alive and will raise His people
death is now harmless apart from Christ
hope is only about improving life now
steps.correctAnswer: Jesus is alive and will raise His people
steps.feedback: Correct. Christ's resurrection is the firstfruits of resurrection hope.
steps.hint: Christ's resurrection is the firstfruits of resurrection hope.

questions.prompt: The empty tomb means...
choices: Jesus truly rose from the dead
Jesus lived on only through His followers' courage
hope became only a memory of His example
questions.correctAnswer: Jesus truly rose from the dead
questions.reviewNote: The resurrection is real, bodily victory over death.

questions.prompt: The risen Jesus appeared to...
choices: real witnesses who saw Him alive
followers whose hope was mainly symbolic
people who could make the story easier to believe
questions.correctAnswer: real witnesses who saw Him alive
questions.reviewNote: The New Testament names witnesses of the risen Christ.

questions.prompt: Jesus' resurrection shows victory over sin and death.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The resurrection confirms Jesus' victory.

questions.prompt: Jesus' ascension means He...
choices: reigns at the Father's right hand
moved away from earth and stopped ruling it
became present only as a memory or idea
questions.correctAnswer: reigns at the Father's right hand
questions.reviewNote: The ascended Christ reigns.

questions.prompt: Before ascending, Jesus sent His disciples to...
choices: be His witnesses
wait until the nations became ready
begin with political reform before witness
questions.reviewNote: Jesus sent His followers as witnesses.

lessons.title: Jesus Will Return
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise hope, judgment, restoration, and faithful living now.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise hope, judgment, restoration, and faithful living now.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Jesus Will Return Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand Jesus' return as hope, judgment, and restoration.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Jesus Will Return
steps.text: This lesson practises hope, judgment, restoration, and faithful living now. God is faithful to His promises, and His people need a true and faithful King. Scripture connection: Matthew 24:36-44; Acts 1:11; Revelation 21:1-5. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: He Will Return
steps.prompt: The angels in Acts 1 said Jesus would...
choices: come again
stay in heaven without future judgment
return secretly only to a few teachers
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus will return personally and visibly.
steps.hint: Jesus will return personally and visibly.

steps.title: No Date Setting
steps.prompt: Jesus taught that people do not know...
choices: the day or hour of His return
whether His promise needs watchfulness
whether people should prepare with faithfulness
steps.correctAnswer: the day or hour of His return
steps.feedback: Correct. Christians should be watchful without date setting.
steps.hint: Christians should be watchful without date setting.

steps.title: Certain Hope
steps.prompt: Jesus' return is certain even though the exact time is not known.
choices: True
False
steps.feedback: Correct. The promise is certain; the timing is not given to us.
steps.hint: The promise is certain; the timing is not given to us.

steps.title: Judgment
steps.prompt: Jesus' return includes...
choices: righteous judgment and final justice
ending evil without judging anyone
a future hope that makes present obedience optional
steps.feedback: Correct. Jesus will judge with perfect righteousness.
steps.hint: Jesus will judge with perfect righteousness.

steps.title: New Creation
steps.prompt: Revelation 21 describes hope as...
choices: God making all things new
God replacing creation with a bodiless hope
creation staying wounded but less painful
steps.feedback: Correct. God will restore His creation and dwell with His people.
steps.hint: God will restore His creation and dwell with His people.

steps.title: Faithful Waiting
steps.prompt: Because Jesus will return, Christians should...
choices: live faithfully, watchfully, and hopefully
delay faithfulness until the future is easier to read
focus on dates more than faithful obedience
steps.feedback: Correct. Hope in Christ's return shapes faithful living now.
steps.hint: Hope in Christ's return shapes faithful living now.

questions.prompt: The angels in Acts 1 said Jesus would...
choices: come again
stay in heaven without future judgment
return secretly only to a few teachers
questions.reviewNote: Jesus will return personally and visibly.

questions.prompt: Jesus taught that people do not know...
choices: the day or hour of His return
whether His promise needs watchfulness
whether people should prepare with faithfulness
questions.correctAnswer: the day or hour of His return
questions.reviewNote: Christians should be watchful without date setting.

questions.prompt: Jesus' return is certain even though the exact time is not known.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The promise is certain; the timing is not given to us.

questions.prompt: Jesus' return includes...
choices: righteous judgment and final justice
ending evil without judging anyone
a future hope that makes present obedience optional
questions.reviewNote: Jesus will judge with perfect righteousness.

questions.prompt: Revelation 21 describes hope as...
choices: God making all things new
God replacing creation with a bodiless hope
creation staying wounded but less painful
questions.reviewNote: God will restore His creation and dwell with His people.

lessons.title: Jesus the Promised Saviour Final Quiz
lessons.teacherSummary: Students complete a scored review quiz for Jesus the Promised Saviour.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students complete a scored review quiz for Jesus the Promised Saviour.

quiz.title: Jesus the Promised Saviour: Final Quiz

questions.prompt: John 1 teaches that the Word became flesh, meaning...
choices: the Son of God truly became human
God revealed truth without truly entering human life
Jesus became God's Son only after His birth
questions.correctAnswer: the Son of God truly became human
questions.reviewNote: The eternal Son truly took on human nature.

questions.prompt: Jesus' birth showed...
choices: God's promised Saviour coming by God's power
faithful people arranging the promise by themselves
political rulers setting the timing of God's promise
questions.correctAnswer: God's promised Saviour coming by God's power
questions.reviewNote: Jesus' birth was God's work and fulfilled His promises.

questions.prompt: Jesus is true God and true man.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The incarnation means the Son of God truly became human without ceasing to be God.

questions.prompt: Jesus announced, 'The kingdom of God is at hand,' and called people to...
choices: repent and believe the gospel
clean up outward habits before coming to God
support God's kingdom without repentance
questions.reviewNote: Jesus called for repentance and faith.

questions.prompt: Repentance means...
choices: turning from sin toward God
feeling sorry while keeping sin protected
changing outward habits while guarding the heart
questions.reviewNote: Repentance includes a real turning to God.

questions.prompt: Faith means trusting Jesus, not merely knowing facts about Him.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Biblical faith includes trust.

questions.prompt: When Jesus healed the paralytic, He also showed authority to...
choices: forgive sins
prove His authority without confronting sin
make people admire power apart from repentance
questions.reviewNote: Jesus connected the healing with His authority to forgive.

questions.prompt: When Jesus calmed the storm, the disciples wondered...
choices: who He was, because wind and sea obeyed Him
whether nature was outside His authority
whether experienced people should guide Him first
questions.correctAnswer: who He was, because wind and sea obeyed Him
questions.reviewNote: The miracle revealed Jesus' authority over creation.

questions.prompt: Jesus' miracles were signs pointing to who He is.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The miracles were not random displays; they revealed His identity.

questions.prompt: A parable is a story Jesus used to...
choices: teach truth about God's kingdom and human hearts
make truth sound clever instead of clear
make stories more important than repentance
questions.correctAnswer: teach truth about God's kingdom and human hearts
questions.reviewNote: Parables use story to teach deep truth.

questions.prompt: Parables often reveal...
choices: how people respond to God's word
that imagination can replace obedience
that understanding truth matters less than enjoying the story
questions.correctAnswer: how people respond to God's word
questions.reviewNote: Parables often expose the heart.

questions.prompt: Jesus' parables call for listening, repentance, faith, and obedience.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Parables are meant to be heard and responded to.

questions.prompt: Jesus said He came not to abolish the Law and Prophets but to...
choices: fulfill them
lower God's standard to outward habits
treat them as old customs with little authority
questions.reviewNote: Jesus fulfills the Law and Prophets.

questions.prompt: Jesus taught that righteousness must go deeper than...
choices: outward appearance alone
the words of the Old Testament
the teaching of the prophets
questions.reviewNote: Jesus exposed heart-level sin and obedience.

questions.prompt: Jesus treated the Old Testament as God's true Word.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Jesus honoured and fulfilled the Scriptures.

questions.prompt: The cross shows that sin is...
choices: so serious that only God's rescue can deal with it
less serious than religious leaders claimed
serious, but solved mainly by human regret
questions.correctAnswer: so serious that only God's rescue can deal with it
questions.reviewNote: The cross shows sin's seriousness and God's rescue.

questions.prompt: Substitution means Jesus...
choices: stood in the place of sinners
suffered beside sinners but not for them
removed guilt without changing loyalty
questions.correctAnswer: stood in the place of sinners
questions.reviewNote: Jesus bore sin for His people.

questions.prompt: At the cross, God's love and justice are both shown.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The cross does not set love against justice.
